mainEntity.name: What if my employer didn't provide hearing protection in West Valley City factories?
mainEntity.text: What if my employer didn't provide hearing protection in West Valley City factories?

acceptedAnswer.text: Utah law requires employers to provide protection at 85-90 dB optional, mandatory above 90 dB per OSHA. Failure constitutes violation; workers can claim negligence in comp proceedings, potentially increasing penalties or benefits.

suggestedAnswer.text: Non-provision breaches OSHA 1910.95 and Utah safety duties, strengthening comp claims for NIHL. In West Valley City manufacturing, document absence via photos/witnesses. Carriers may argue 'comparative fault' if you worked without; courts apportion. Sue for full loss plus safety violations—attorneys counter employer defenses effectively.[2][6]

name: Employer Failed to Provide Hearing Protection in Utah
